# Atom Archetype

> Atom Archetype is a Maven archetype that generates Java 21 and Spring Boot 4 multi-module applications with Domain-Driven Design, explicit tenant context, secure HTTP defaults, MyBatis-Plus, Flyway, and optional Redis.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype
Maven coordinates: io.github.archetom:atom-archetype
Current stable release: 2.0.0 (Maven Central)
Legacy Maven Central release: 1.1.0 (Spring Boot 3.5 architecture)
License: MIT

## Start here

- [English README](https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype/blob/main/README.md): value proposition, stable generation command, architecture summary, security defaults, and compatibility.
- [Chinese README](https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype/blob/main/README.zh-CN.md): Simplified Chinese project overview and quick start.
- [Getting started](https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype/blob/main/docs/getting-started.md): prerequisites, generation, local infrastructure, startup, testing, and production authentication.
- [Architecture](https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype/blob/main/docs/architecture.md): module boundaries, request flow, tenant isolation, transactions, events, persistence, and cache ports.
- [Naming conventions](https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype/blob/main/docs/naming-conventions.md): canonical names for API, domain, application, persistence, errors, and use-case events.
- [Upgrade guide](https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype/blob/main/docs/upgrade-guide.md): safe migration from the legacy 1.1.0 architecture to 2.0.
- [Release checklist](https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype/blob/main/docs/releasing.md): signing, Central validation, explicit Portal publication, tags, and post-release versioning.
- [Troubleshooting](https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype/blob/main/docs/troubleshooting.md): Maven, Java, profiles, 401/403, tenant, Flyway, Redis, Testcontainers, and Velocity issues.
- [Changelog](https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): release status and breaking changes.
- [Security policy](https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype/blob/main/SECURITY.md): supported versions and private vulnerability reporting.
- [Agent instructions](https://github.com/Archetom/atom-archetype/blob/main/AGENTS.md): repository-specific editing and verification rules.

## Stable release generation

```bash
mvn -B org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-archetype-plugin:3.4.1:generate \
  -DarchetypeGroupId=io.github.archetom \
  -DarchetypeArtifactId=atom-archetype \
  -DarchetypeVersion=2.0.0 \
  -DgroupId=com.example.orders \
  -DartifactId=orders-service \
  -Dpackage=com.example.orders \
  -Dversion=1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
```

## Key architecture facts

- Generated modules: api, domain, shared, application, infra/external, infra/security, infra/persistence, infra/rest, infra/facade, and start.
- Domain is framework-neutral and never depends on application, API, shared, or infrastructure modules.
- Application use cases receive AuthenticatedCaller and pass a required TenantId to repositories and tenant-scoped caches.
- CommandServiceTemplate and QueryServiceTemplate execute typed ServiceOperation lifecycles.
- Domain errors map to application errors; current names are ApplicationException, NonRetryableApplicationException, and UseCaseOperation.
- Flyway migrations are the only schema source. MyBatis-Plus implements repository adapters and optimistic locking.
- Cache/event side effects use AfterCommitExecutor. In-process events are not a durable outbox.
- Redis is disabled by default; NoOpCacheService preserves behavior without Redis.
- Business APIs are fail-closed. Trusted X-Dev-* identity headers are available only in explicitly enabled dev/test profiles and never grant client-supplied roles.

## Maintainer constraints

- Template code is under src/main/resources/archetype-resources/ and contains intentional Velocity variables such as ${package}, ${artifactId}, ${rootArtifactId}, and ${version}.
- Use #set( $dollar = '$' ) plus ${dollar}{ENV_VAR} when a filtered template must emit a literal Spring ${ENV_VAR} placeholder.
- Update src/main/resources/META-INF/maven/archetype-metadata.xml when adding files or modules that are not covered by an existing file set.
- Update clean.sh when adding, removing, or renaming removable sample files.
- After a template change, run make install, make demo, compile the generated project with `sh ./mvnw`, and run `CI=true sh ./mvnw test` when Docker is available.

## Optional generated-project documentation

- The generated project also contains a docs/ directory with application-oriented architecture, configuration, object-layering, usage, and testing guidance. Root repository documentation is authoritative for maintaining and upgrading the archetype itself.
